sections.title: Limitations / Miscellaneous
sections.body:
PPROACH & LANDING
• Steep approach approved aircraft and qualified crew shall be used.
• Steep approach procedures and performance shall be used.
• Fractional Ops performance is PROHIBITED.
• See OM-A 8.3.28.3 Steep Approach Operations, OM-D 2.1.1.5.6 and OM-D 2.1.3.4.6.

EGLC PF / PM ASSIGNMENT
• BD-700: LHS shall be PF for take-off and landing.
• All other fleets: PF assignment for take-off shall be at the discretion of the PIC. PF shall hold a current AHQ.
For Approach:
• PF shall hold current AHQ and Steep Approach PF qualifications.
• PM shall hold as a minimum, a current Steep Approach PM qualification.

LONDON, UK
LONDON CITY
40-10-2P2

-
WIND LIMITATIONS

Max CROSSWIND Component for LANDING:
EMB-505:
28 KTS
CE-560XLS:
24 KTS
CE-680A:
30 KTS
BD-100:
20 KTS
CL-600:
20 KTS
BD-700:
15 KTS

Max TAILWIND Component for LANDING:
EMB-505:
5 KTS
CE-680A:
5 KTS
CE-560XLS:
0 (zero) KTS
BD-100:
0 (zero) KTS
CL-600:
0 (zero) KTS
BD-700:
0 (zero) KTS

Note: EGLC normally operates to reduce tailwind component (TWC) to zero if possible; however, a slight
TWC is sometimes experienced with a light Southerly wind. Up to and including 5 KTS, TWC can be
accepted on landing if specifically permitted by OM-B / AFM and when performance permits.

Max Wind for TAXIING:
All fleets:
50 KTS

Max CROSSWIND Component for TAKE-OFF:
As per OM-B.

DIVERSION OF FLIGHT

WARNING:
It is PROHIBITED to nominate EGLC as a nominated diversion aerodrome.

Diversions for customer/crew preference to EGLC shall be avoided, except if coordinated with Flight Dispatch
or the Duty Manager (via Hotline).

RUNWAY SURFACE (as per AIP AD 2.20)
• Operations by all aircraft shall be permitted only when the runway is DRY, or if WET devoid of other than small
areas of water not exceeding depth of 3 mm.
• Operations will be PROHIBITED when the runway is CONTAMINATED:
o by ICE or SLUSH to a depth exceeding 3 mm, or
o DRY SNOW to a depth of 10 mm, or
o the reported friction measurement is reported as worse than 'medium' provided that this condition
shall not apply if there is an appropriate entry covering operation from contaminated runways
contained in the relevant flight manual.

LONDON, UK
LONDON CITY
40-10-2P3

-

sections.title: Overview
sections.body:
ondon City airport is a busy civil airport located in East London and is Category C due to required Steep Approach
training.
The airport also needs special consideration due to the relatively short runway, close proximity obstacles, possible
magnetic anomalies and noise.
Refer to Jeppesen Airport Briefing pages 40-1P through 40-1P2 for additional information.

Airport overview

LONDON, UK
LONDON CITY
40-10-2P4

-

Refer to Jeppesen Airport Briefing pages 40-1P through 40-1P2 for additional information.

sections.title: Operational procedures — General
sections.body:
he airport is surrounded by close in buildings / obstacles, hence the need for the steep approach. Expect possible
low-level turbulence and windshear in strong winds.

lines: he airport is surrounded by close in buildings / obstacles, hence the need for the steep approach. Expect possible
low-level turbulence and windshear in strong winds.

sections.title: Arrival procedures
sections.body:
.1. GENERAL
Under normal conditions, an ILS/LOC approach shall be conducted, refer to 9.2 ILS/LOC APPROACHES.
Whenever the ILS/LOC is unavailable by NOTAM or AIP Supplement, a visual approach is required. Under this
condition, refer to 9.3 VISUAL APPROACH.

Final RWY 27

Final RWY 09

LONDON, UK
LONDON CITY
40-10-2P5

-
9.2. ILS/LOC APPROACHES

MINIMA:

As per Jeppesen

PERFORMANCE:
Steep approach performance shall be used.
Check tailwind carefully - ensure any Fleet specific/AFM tailwind limitations are
adhered to.

PROCEDURE:
As per Jeppesen.
Steep approach procedures shall be used

CAUTION:
ODLEG transition does not connect directly to the ILS 09 and the distance from ODLEG to the Localiser is less than
1.9nm so careful planning and monitoring of Flight Director modes is required.

LONDON, UK
LONDON CITY
40-10-2P6

-
9.3. VISUAL APPROACH

WARNING
A visual approach is only to be used when ILS/LOC approach is not available, this will be indicated by NOTAM
and/or AIP Supplement (due to navaids being unserviceable).
In all other conditions, an ILS/LOC approach shall be conducted (as per 9.2 ILS/LOC APPROACHES).

MINIMA:

Ceiling:
2000ft

Visibility:
6KM

PAPI must be operative.

PERFORMANCE:
Steep approach performance shall be used.
Check tailwind carefully - ensure any Fleet specific/AFM tailwind limitations are
adhered to.

PROCEDURE:
Steep approach procedures shall be used.
Refer to chart 49-10 VISUAL APPROACH PROFILE (CAT A, B & C).  This chart is contained
in the TAXI tab (and not the APP tab) on the iPad FD Pro App.

Note: on consultation with EGLC ATC, crew may expect to commence the Visual
approach from 2000ft and not the 1500ft/1600ft shown on Jepp 49-10.  This is partially
traffic flow with THAMES RADAR and partially due to Noise Abatement strategies.  This
equates to 3.4nm from THOLD.

Distances on this chart are based on the DME which MAY NOT be available.  In this
situation, make use of the Speed/Rate of Descent tables on the appropriate ILS Jepp
Chart.

Configure early and make a smooth but positive attitude selection for the final descent
to avoid being high.

Note the requirement to hold the required visual references before final visual descent.

Be prepared to GO AROUND.  This is not a usual operational mode and errors in the
entry and maintenance of the visual steep descent may be encountered.  Given the
short final sector length, an unstable approach may result.

MISSED APPROACH:
(Including a Balked Landing). Following consultation with EGLC ATC, the following
procedures can be expected:

• EGLC may provide specific Instructions to aircraft on a Visual Approach in the event
of a missed approach.  In the event no instructions are received:

• RWY 27:  Follow the ILS/LOC published missed approach.  If the ILSR is unavailable
on RWY 27, use LON DME.  This is shown on the Jepp 41-2 Chart.

• RWY 09:  Climb straight ahead (track 092º) to 2000ft and follow ATC instructions.
Expect to be handed to THAMES RADAR (DIRECTOR) as soon as safely possible.

LONDON, UK
LONDON CITY
40-10-2P7

-

sections.title: Departure procedures
sections.body:
0.1. GENERAL
As per Jeppesen.
Short runway - consider static take-off.
CAUTION:
Be aware of noise monitoring – use noise abatement take-off.
EGLC is a level bust hotspot. Low level off procedures in accordance with OM-B should be followed.

lines: Be aware of noise monitoring – use noise abatement take-off.
EGLC is a level bust hotspot. Low level off procedures in accordance with OM-B should be followed.

sections.title: Ground procedures
sections.body:
he Apron is very limited for space. A marshaller must be in attendance for all Apron Movements and Engine Starts.

MAGNETIC ANOMALIES:
Due to ferrous material in the RWY structure and the close proximity metallic items at the Jet Centre apron, be vigilant
to magnetic anomalies. The threat is most apparent at the holding point for RWY 27 (especially when facing North at
holding point M) but can occur at the Jet centre apron.
Measures to minimise magnetic problems include using slew mode if able or facing East instead of North at holding
point M.
Any HDG discrepancies shall be resolved before departure is initiated.

NOISE SENSITIVE:
Be aware of APU time limitations - see Jepps 40-4. Consider requesting GPU.

Measures to minimise magnetic problems include using slew mode if able or facing East instead of North at holding
Any HDG discrepancies shall be resolved before departure is initiated.
Be aware of APU time limitations - see Jepps 40-4. Consider requesting GPU.
